title: LRC - JUIT
content:
PLAGIARISM TOOLS @ JUIT About Plagiarism: Plagiarism is an academic offense which can ruin academic and  research reputation of any academicians or the parent institute. It degrades  academic standard and creates a negative impact on the professional front as  well. It is an act of academic misconduct which can broadly be defined as using  another person’s words or ideas without giving credit to that person. Use of  Internet in day to day lives makes plagiarism issue a very serious and critical  one. Plagiarism through ignorance happens sometimes but in a majority of the  cases plagiarism happens deliberately, even poor vigilance can also offer a  mean to plagiarize. Therefore, the purpose of the document is to establish and  ensure a framework, based on ethical guidelines issued by the University Grants  Commission (UGC) through its notification ‘Promotion of Academic Integrity and  Prevention of Plagiarism in Higher Educational Institutions, 2018’ Regulations  dated July 31, 2018. 1 UGC Notification on ‘Promotion of Academic Integrity and    Prevention of Plagiarism in Higher Educational Institutions, 2018’, dated    July 31, 2018. Link 2 JUIT Office Order – Promotion of Academic Integrity and    Prevention of Plagiarism in Higher Educational Institutions Link 3 Self Plagiarism -     clarification of UGC Link 4 JUIT policy on ‘Code of Ethics for Research’ Link 5 JUIT Notification Link 6 Plagiarism Report Request Form Link JUIT is making use of software to detect plagiarism in project  reports, dissertations and theses. Currently, JUIT has been using following  tools for curbing plagiarism TURNITIN: Turnitin is a leading anti-plagiarism software used at JUIT to promote academic integrity and originality in student submissions. It helps educators and students identify potential instances of plagiarism by comparing submitted work against a vast database of scholarly publications, websites, and student papers. Turnitin  (login page – https://www.turnitin.com/login_page.asp?lang=en_us ) Guides  & Tutorials: Turnitin  user guide for faculty: ( PDF ) How to  Create Your Turnitin Instructor Profile ( PDF ) DrillBit NFLIBNET Centre is providing DrillBit-Extreme ‘Plagiarism Detection Software’ to Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) under the ShodhShudhi  Programme.
  JUIT has the access to DrillBit under the same. DrillBit (login page – https://www.drillbitplagiarismcheck.com/auth/login ) Guides  & Tutorials: DrillBit  user guide for faculty: ( PDF ) How to  Create Your DrillBit Instructor Profile ( PDF ) SIMILARITY PLAGIARISM VERIFICATION PROCESS AT  JUIT: The  plagiarism form hard copy is available at the circulation counter of the LRC,  or you can download it from the LRC website in PDF format. STEP 1: Please  fill in the form with complete required information and submit it to the LRC  Circulation Counter for further verification process. (Incomplete form shall  not be entrained) STEP 2: The  guide/supervisor will send the following documents on the given email: plagcheck.juit@gmail.com along with the following documents. Document 1: Complete thesis/dissertation  from title page to last page (ready to submit to the University) in PDF/Doc  format. Document 2: Title page, abstract and  chapters only in Word/Doc format only (exclude preliminary pages and  references). Kindly Note: Please use this facility only in case, of  final submissions of theses/dissertations/reports etc. of research scholar/post  graduate students, to the university. Checking of the draft thesis/chapters,  articles please approach the guide/supervisor or concerned faculty of your  department. Checking of similarity index of Under  Graduate level courses shall be done by the concerned guide supervisor or  faculty members only. Need  Help? Please  contact us at following email/contact numbers: plagcheck.juit@gmail.com , or suresh.kumar@juitsolan.in , vipin.sharma@juitsolan.in Ph: +91-1792-239  382 (Ext 382), +91-1792-239 214 (Ext 214), +91-1792-239 286 (Ext 286) or you  may visit the library for any assistance.
